info.title: Retriever API
info.description: The Retriever API allows you to submit device return orders to Retriever and view their status. If you have any questions or issues, please email us at help@helloretriever.com. We also provide a browsable API which allows you to explore and test the API. If you are logged into the Retriever portal, you can use your browser to visit any URL from the documentation below to see its return value and/or submit requests. Note that the browsable API shows real data from your account and allows you to submit real device return orders; you will be billed for any order you submit. # API Versions ## V1 Supports laptop returns from US-based employees. Click here ## V2 Update to support the addition of cell phone and monitor returns from US-based employees and laptop and cell phone returns from Canada-based employees. Click here *NOTE : For returns from Canada-based employees the return address for the device must be an address within the United States. # Testing If you would like to test submitting orders via the API without being billed and without the order being shipped, you can submit orders and then manually cancel them via your portal dashboard within 30 minutes. Note that orders are final and cannot be cancelled after 30 minutes. # Rate Limits To ensure reliability and prevent abuse, API read requests (fetching device return order information) are limited to 60 per minute and 500 per day. Write requests (submitting device return orders) are limited to 600 per minute and 5000 per day. A `429 Too Many Requests` response will be returned if the limits are exceeded. These limits should be sufficient for most API usage; please contact us if you have a need for a higher volume of API calls.

get.description:
Gets all device return orders for your organization, sorted by date created (newest first).
                    Results are paginated using a cursor with up to 50 per page.

post.description:
Creates a device return order. Monitor and laptop returns can be created for US-based employees and laptop returns can be created for Canada-based employees.
Requests to include a cell phone as part of the return are available for laptop returns only.


**Important note:** Addresses provided via the API are not validated. The addresses you provide will be used as is.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A unique value identifying this warehouse device.

return_recipient_name.description: The return recipient name to print on the shipping label.

return_address_company.description: The return recipient company to print on the shipping label.

return_address_line_1.description:
The first line of the return address (typically the street address), or a full, comma-separated address.

If providing the first line of the address, `return_address_city`, `return_address_state`, and `return_address_zip` are
required (`return_address_line_2` remains optional).

If providing a full address, it is best to use the format `1 Main St, New York, NY 10001` or
`1 Main St, Apt 200, New York, NY 10001`. We will attempt to parse any full address, but cannot guarantee
accuracy - especially if other formats are used. It is highly recommended to provide the address in separate fields.

return_address_city.description: Optional if a full address is provided in `return_address_line_1`. Required otherwise.

return_address_state.description: Optional if a full address is provided in `return_address_line_1`. Required otherwise.

return_address_zip.description: Optional if a full address is provided in `return_address_line_1`. Required otherwise.

display_name.description: Used in email communications with the employee.

notification_emails.description:
Optional email addresses for updates on the status of this
                                                          device return.

id.description: The unique identifier for the device deployment.

device_id.description: The device id of the device being deployed.

purchaser_email.description: The email address of the user who placed this order.

created_via_api.description: Whether this order was submitted via the API.

shipping_speed.description:
Shipping speed for device deployment order.

* `standard_shipping` - standard_shipping
* `2nd_day` - 2nd_day
* `next_day` - next_day

include_return_label.description: Include a return label with the deployment.

return_order_type.description:
Type of return order, Required if include_return_label is true.

* `reprovision` - Reprovision
* `disposal` - Disposal
* `requires_service` - Requires service
* `deliver_to_another_address` - Deliver to another address

created_at.description: The date and time when the device deployment was created, in ISO 8601 format.

employee_info.description: The personal information of the employee.

shipment.description: The shipment details for the device.

display_name.description: The display name of the company deploying the device.

notification_emails.description: The notification email addresses for the company deploying the device.

request_disposal.description: Send to Retriever for data destruction. If TRUE, Only display_name is required in company_info data

serial_number.description: The serial number of the device being returned.

ticket_id.description: The ticket ID of the device being returned.

purchaser_email.description:
The email address of the user who placed this
            order. If submitted via API, this will be the email address associated with the API key.

codd.description: URL of the certificate, if disposal service is requested and completed.

shipments.description:
Device return shipments associated with
            this order. Currently 1 shipment per order, but multiple shipments (for example, two laptops and a monitor)
            may be supported in the future.

ram.description: The amount of RAM installed on the device, in MB or GB

disk.description: The total disk storage capacity of the device, in GB or TB

processor.description: The type and model of the CPU or processor in the device

recipient_source.description: The origin or source from where the device was received into the warehouse

operating_system.description:
The operating system installed on the device

* `linux` - Linux
* `macos` - macOS
* `other` - Other
* `windows` - Windows

os_version.description: The version number of the installed operating system

status.description:
The current status of the device

* `administrative_hold` - Administrative Hold
* `delivered_address` - Delivered For Disposal
* `deployed` - Deployed
* `deployment_initiated` - Deployment In Transit
* `deployment_requested` - Deployment Requested
* `device_received` - Device Received
* `disposal_initiated` - Disposal Initiated
* `disposed` - Disposed
* `in_repair` - In Repair
* `input_required` - Input Required
* `label_generated` - Label Generated
* `legal_hold` - Legal Hold
* `lost_in_transit` - Lost In Transit
* `provisioning` - Provisioning
* `ready_for_deployment` - Ready For Deployment
* `requires_service` - Requires Service
* `returned` - Returned
* `return_initiated` - Return Initiated
* `returned_to_vendor` - Returned To Vendor
* `retrieval_initiated` - Retrieval In Transit
* `to_be_retired` - To Be Retired

current_location.description: The physical or organizational location where the device is stored or used

asset_tag.description: Tag assigned to the device for asset management purposes

has_charger.description: Indicates if the device includes a charger

customer_name.description: The name of the customer who owns the device

initial_deployment.description: The date when the device was first deployed

The first line of the return address (typically the street address), or a full, comma-separated address.

If providing the first line of the address, `return_address_city`, `return_address_state`, and `return_address_zip` are
required (`return_address_line_2` remains optional).

If providing a full address, it is best to use the format `1 Main St, New York, NY 10001` or

accuracy - especially if other formats are used. It is highly recommended to provide the address in separate fields.

The current status of the device shipment.
        status list: delivered_for_disposal, forward_to_address, awaiting_shipment, deployed, device_shipped, deactivated

The email address of the user who placed this
            order. If submitted via API, this will be the email address associated with the API key.

codd.description: URL of the certificate, if disposal service is requested and completed.

shipments.description:
Device return shipments associated with
            this order. Currently 1 shipment per order, but multiple shipments (for example, two laptops and a monitor)
            may be supported in the future.

send_to_retriever.description: if device sent to retriever for disposal

send_to_retriever_warehousing.description: if device sent to retriever for warehousing

legal_hold.description: no. of days for device to be on legal hold

status.description:
The status of this device return shipment. A shipment
        progresses in the following order: `"box_pending_shipment"` → `"box_in_transit"` → `"box_delivered"` →
        `"return_in_transit"` → `"return_complete"` or `"received_for_disposal"` or `"received_at_warehouse"` or `"received_at_storage_location"`. 
        Note that a shipment won't necessarily go through every state. If the device return is sent to Retriever for data destruction, 
        the status will be `"received_for_disposal"`. If the device return is sent to Retriever warehouse, the status will be `"received_at_warehouse"`. 
        If the device return is sent to a storage location, the status will be `"received_at_storage_location"`. 
        The status will be `"deactivated"` if the device return is deactivated by you or your team through the portal, 
        by your employee, or by the Retriever team in the event that the device has already been returned or this device return is no longer needed.

insurance_amount.description:
The amount of insurance coverage purchased for this
                                                           shipment in whole dollars. `null` if no insurance.

device_type.description:
Reserved for future expansion. Currently all device returns are
                                                    for laptops.

* `laptop` - laptop

request_charger.description:
Set for laptops, and `null` for other devices where not
                                                          applicable.

request_cell_phone.description:
Set for laptops, and `null` or `False` for other devices where not
                                                          applicable.

outbound_tracking.description:
Tracking number for the empty box shipped to the
                                                            employee. Assigned when the order ships.

outbound_carrier.description:
The shipping carrier for the outbound box. Assigned when
                                                     the order ships.

return_tracking.description:
Tracking number for the device return shipped by the
                                                          employee. Assigned when the order ships.

return_carrier.description:
The shipping carrier for the device return. Assigned when
                                                   the order ships.

email.description: Email address of the employee for deployment notification.

name.description: The employee name to print on the shipping label.

address_line_1.description:
The first line of the employee's address (typically the street address), or a full, comma-separated address.

If providing the first line of the address, `address_city`, `address_state`, and `address_zip` are required
(`address_line_2` remains optional).

If providing a full address, it is best to use the format `1 Main St, New York, NY 10001` or
`1 Main St, Apt 200, New York, NY 10001`. We will attempt to parse any full address, but cannot guarantee
accuracy - especially if other formats are used. It is highly recommended to provide the address in separate fields.

address_city.description: Optional if a full address is provided in `address_line_1`. Required otherwise.

address_state.description: Optional if a full address is provided in `address_line_1`. Required otherwise.

address_zip.description: Optional if a full address is provided in `address_line_1`. Required otherwise.

detail.description:
Error message describing why we were unable
                                                                      to bill your account for this order.

next.description: The URL of the next page of results, if any.

previous.description: The URL of the previous page of results, if any.

next.description: The URL of the next page of results, if any.

previous.description: The URL of the previous page of results, if any.

next.description: The URL of the next page of results, if any.

previous.description: The URL of the previous page of results, if any.

num_of_credits.description: total number of credit of device.

ReturnOrderTypeEnum.description:
* `reprovision` - Reprovision
* `disposal` - Disposal
* `requires_service` - Requires service
* `deliver_to_another_address` - Deliver to another address

API Key.description:
Authentication is performed using an API key, which can be obtained by any admin
in the Retriever portal. When making an API call, the key should be provided
in the Authorization header as follows:

`Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>`

Note that each user has their own API key, and that key will stop
working if the user is removed from your team in the Retriever portal.
Make sure you check and update any keys used in production before removing a user.

tags.name: Deployment Orders
tags.description: The Deployment Orders API is designed to allow companies to manage their laptop inventory and automate the deployment of laptops stored with Retriever as part of our warehouse and deployment service.
